result.summary: MongoDB exposes two documented HTTP APIs (Atlas Administration API and Atlas App Services Admin API), an Atlas CLI plus legacy `mongocli`, and an official MongoDB MCP server; authentication is required via Atlas service-account OAuth client credentials, legacy Atlas API key pairs, or an App Services bearer session token minted from Atlas API keys.

atlas_service_account.label: Atlas service account client ID and client secret
atlas_service_account.setup: In [Get Started with the Atlas Administration API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/configure-api-access/), go to **Applications** in your Atlas organization, click **Add new Service Account**, assign organization/project roles, create it, and copy the client secret when shown. Use the client ID and secret to mint an OAuth token with the client-credentials flow at `POST https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token`.

atlas_api_key.label: Atlas Administration API public/private API key pair
atlas_api_key.setup: In [Get Started with the Atlas Administration API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/configure-api-access/), create programmatic API access for your Atlas organization or project and save the **Public Key** and **Private Key**. Atlas documents these as the legacy API-key method for Atlas Administration API access via HTTP Digest authentication.

app_services_session_token.label: Atlas App Services Admin API session access token
app_services_session_token.setup: First create Atlas programmatic API keys using [Get Started with the Atlas Administration API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/configure-api-access/). Then call the App Services admin login endpoint described in [MongoDB Atlas App Services Admin API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/api/doc/atlas-app-services-admin-api-v3/) with JSON `{"username":"<Public API Key>","apiKey":"<Private API Key>"}` at `POST https://services.cloud.mongodb.com/api/admin/v3.0/auth/providers/mongodb-cloud/login` to receive an `access_token`. Use the returned `refresh_token` with `POST https://services.cloud.mongodb.com/api/admin/v3.0/auth/session` to renew it.

requiredHeaders.name: Accept
requiredHeaders.description: Versioned media type shown in the API reference example.

surfaces.name: MongoDB Atlas App Services Admin API

packages.runtimeHint: package name inferred from docs command; install docs linked from Atlas CLI documentation
